items.title: Why Olise can be one of France's very best - Clichy
items.summary: Former France international Gael Clichy explains why Bayern Munich's France forward Michael Olise could be the breakout star of this summer's World Cup.

items.summary: Max Rushden is joined by Barry Glendenning , Lars Sivertsen, Mark Langdon, Ewan Murray and Jack Snape to preview Groups A-D including Scotland and Brazil Rate, review, share on Apple Podcasts and join the conversation on email . items.title: Man Utd star Noussair Mazraoui forced off just 29 minutes into Morocco’s final World Cup warm-up before facing Brazil
items.summary: NOUSSAIR MAZRAOUI has suffered an injury scare just DAYS before Morocco's World Cup opener. The Atlas Lions are due to face Brazil on June 13 but Mazraoui could be in a race against time to be fit for kick-off after coming off in their final warm-up fixture. items.title: Reset or repeat? Have England really changed after Ashes thrashing?
items.summary: Following all the pre-match talk about whether England's style would change in the wake of the Ashes debacle, a quick scour of the Lord's scorecard post their rout of New Zealand would suggest more of the same.

items.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 1
